topic: Unit 5: A2 Themes (Young People in Society; Our Place in a Changing World)
subject: french

items.correct: Voluntary unpaid work for a cause
items.why: Students assume it means paid work; le bénévolat is volunteering, a key form of youth engagement.

items.q: 'La mondialisation' translates as which English term?
items.why: Students mistranslate it; la mondialisation is globalisation, a central theme of 'a changing world'.

items.q: What is meant by 'l'intégration' in social debate?
items.correct: How newcomers become part of wider society
items.why: Students confuse the maths sense; here intégration concerns immigration and social cohesion.

items.correct: Voluntary, unpaid work for a cause
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Voluntary, unpaid work for a cause. “Paid full-time employment” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: 'La mondialisation' is the French term for which process?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Globalisation. “Privatisation” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which theme best fits 'notre place dans un monde en mutation'?
items.correct: Globalisation and immigration in a changing world
items.why: It's Globalisation and immigration in a changing world. “French verb conjugation” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: How newcomers become part of wider society
items.why: The answer examiners want is: How newcomers become part of wider society. “A maths operation only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
